humanities_role_explanation: Uses art history images from six cultural traditions as domain content AND is specifically designed for cultural art interpretation and understanding
benchmark_modalities_explanation: Multi-modal evaluation combining artwork images with bilingual text generation - VLMs receive artwork images and generate bilingual (Chinese-English) art critiques covering L1-L5 levels of cultural understanding
analytical_tasks_explanation: Five-level analytical hierarchy from visual perception (L1-L2) to deep cultural interpretation (L3-L5): L1 visual perception, L2 technical analysis, L3 cultural symbolism, L4 historical context, L5 philosophical aesthetics. Tasks require interpreting culture-specific concepts, identifying artistic techniques, and contextualizing within art-historical traditions

general_purpose_benchmark.note: Evaluates general cultural understanding capabilities across multiple VLMs from different providers

evaluates_generative_llm.note: Evaluates 15 VLMs including GPT-4o, GPT-5, Claude-Sonnet-4.5, Claude-Opus-4.5, Gemini-2.5-Pro, Qwen3-VL-235B, Llama4-Scout, and others

llm_is_primary_system.note: VLMs are the primary systems being benchmarked for cultural art interpretation capabilities

arts_humanities_data.note: Visual Arts and Performing Arts, History, Language and Linguistics - covers 6,695 artwork images across six cultural traditions with expert art critiques

quantitative_metrics.note: Multiple metrics: tri-tier framework with automated metrics (DCR, CSA, CDS, LQS), rubric-based scoring (1-5 scale), human expert calibration, ICC agreement measures

english_language.note: Paper written in English, benchmark uses bilingual Chinese-English critiques

summary: VULCA-BENCH introduces a tri-tier evaluation framework for assessing Vision-Language Models' cultural understanding in art interpretation across six cultural traditions. The framework combines automated metrics, single-judge rubric scoring, and human expert calibration to evaluate cultural competence from visual description (L1-L2) to deep cultural interpretation (L3-L5).
key_findings: VLMs show degraded performance from visual description (L1-L2) to cultural interpretation (L3-L5), with Critique Depth (σ=0.56) and Cultural Alignment (σ=0.48) being most discriminative. 13 of 15 models exhibit systematic bias favoring Western art over non-Western traditions (Cohen's d = -0.74, p < 0.001). Automated metrics and judge scoring measure different constructs (ICC < 0.2).
paper_name: JOKER 2025: Computational Task on Pun and Wordplay Translation
